title: service-accounts — validation rules
description: Two custom validation rules published by ServiceAccountServiceProvider::boot(). Both operate on scalar string input; both are stateless. Consumers wire them via #[Validation] attribute on Data-class properties.

rules.name: valid_service_account_name
rules.description: Enforces the naming policy from `service-accounts.naming.*` config: max length (default 128), regex pattern (default `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9_-]*[a-z0-9]$`), uniqueness within the SA's `application_id` (scoped by the passed argument). Rejects whitespace, uppercase, Unicode, and punctuation to keep names URL-safe + shell-safe.

params.name: application_id
params.description: The application_id under which the name must be unique. Passed at validation time from the request context (never trusted from user input).

rules.default_message: The :attribute must be lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens or underscores, 3-128 characters, and unique within the application.

rules.name: valid_kid_format
rules.description: Validates the shape of a `signer_kid` string against `service-accounts.kid.format` config. Default `uuid` = UUIDv4 (with optional application prefix when `include_application_prefix=true`); `opaque` = exactly 32 lowercase alphanumeric chars. Additionally verifies existence + enablement via a fresh query against `auth::jwt_signing_keys` (this is where the runtime FK check happens — the schema does not enforce it).
rules.default_message: The :attribute must reference an existing, enabled signing key.

usage_from_data_class: Data classes composed with `CreateServiceAccountInput`, `UpdateServiceAccountInput` automatically wire these rules via the #[Validation] attribute on each property; consumers rarely reference the rules by name.
